title: Feed username resolution and profile-gated posting/commenting

requirements.summary: Show author profile usernames (with English fallbacks) for posts and comments in the feed UI, including avatar initials derived from usernames.
acceptanceCriteria: In `frontend/src/components/FeedPostCard.tsx`, the post header shows the author’s profile `name` (username) instead of `post.author.toString().slice(0, 10)`.
In `frontend/src/components/FeedPostCard.tsx`, each rendered comment shows the comment author’s profile `name` (username) instead of `comment.author.toString().slice(0, 10)`.
If a profile lookup fails or returns null, the UI shows a clear fallback label in English (e.g., "Unknown user") rather than a truncated principal.
Avatar fallbacks use initials derived from the username when available (for both post and comment author avatars).

file_operations.description: Replace truncated principal rendering for post/comment authors with a reusable author display (name + avatar fallback initials) powered by cached profile lookups; include an English fallback label (e.g., "Unknown user") when profile is missing or fetch fails.
Add small UI helpers for deriving display initials from a username and providing consistent English fallback labels for missing/unknown users.

requirements.summary: Add a shared React Query hook to fetch and cache arbitrary user profiles by principal, and reuse it to resolve usernames in the feed without render loops.
acceptanceCriteria: A new hook is added in `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts` to fetch `getUserProfile(principal)` and cache it with a stable queryKey that includes the principal string (e.g., `['userProfile', principalStr]`).
`FeedPostCard` uses the new hook (or equivalent shared query logic) to display usernames for the post author and comment authors without introducing infinite re-render loops.
User-facing error handling remains in English and does not crash the feed if a profile fetch fails.

file_operations.description: Add a new React Query hook (e.g., `useGetUserProfile(principal)`) that calls the backend capability `getUserProfile`, caches results under a stable queryKey including the principal string, and is safely disabled until the actor is ready. (Authorization component context: this aligns with profile access patterns; Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.)

Refactor post/comment author rendering to use the new `useGetUserProfile` hook via a dedicated child component (one hook call per rendered author) to stay within React hook rules and avoid infinite re-render loops.

requirements.summary: Prevent posting/commenting in the UI when the current user has no profile, and display readable English errors (including backend "profile required" traps) via normalized messaging.
acceptanceCriteria: When the authenticated user has no profile, the app flow continues to block interaction via the existing profile setup modal (no route/page renders behind it).

If any posting/commenting UI can still render (edge cases), the post composer submit and comment submit actions are disabled and show an English message instructing the user to create a profile first.
If the backend returns the new "profile required" error, the frontend displays a readable English toast/error message (not raw traps).

file_operations.description: Use the existing current-user profile query to disable the "Post" action when the authenticated user has no profile (edge-case protection), and show an English message instructing the user to create a profile first. Also normalize backend errors into readable English text before showing toasts. (Authorization component context: this complements the profile setup flow; Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.)

Add edge-case UI gating for comment submission when the current user has no profile: disable comment submit and show an English message prompting profile creation; ensure any backend trap/reject errors are normalized to readable English before display.

Update post/comment mutation error handling to use the shared IC error normalization utility so new backend "profile required" trap messages (and other canister rejects) are displayed as readable English toasts.
